reasoning: Peer set is silver/grey-dominant metallic helmets with cool color temperature, bold 1-2px outlines, and moderate detail density. Candidate (boss key + staff) deviates on multiple axes: (1) COLOR TEMPERATURE — candidate is warm gold/yellow dominant, peers are cool grey-silver; no shared neutral mid-tone anchors the families together. (2) PALETTE OVERLAP — candidate palette is almost entirely warm yellows and golds; peers use this hue only as a minor accent (one gold helmet), making candidate look like it belongs to a different game tier or rarity system. (3) OUTLINE WEIGHT — candidate lines read as thinner and more filigree-like compared to the chunky, confident outlines on peer helmets; creates 'delicate jewelry vs sturdy armor' split. (4) DETAIL DENSITY — candidate key sprite is packed with fine interior linework (teeth, bow detail, chain links on staff); peers are clean silhouette-first reads. (5) CHARM LEVEL — peers have a compact, toy-like solidity that reads as 'cute game icon'; candidate's elongated key silhouette and busy interior feel more like a realistic inventory prop, reducing tonal cohesion with the peer family. Shadow direction (top-left) and count (single drop shadow) are consistent. Rendering style is pixel art throughout. Proportion critique waived per item-type exemption. Net: passes on structure, fails on palette/temperature/density — marginal, requires palette harmonization pass.
